texto: Considering the extravagance of my expectations, the flying experience was bound to be a disappointment.
explicacao: Citação de Edna Robinson sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “Considering the extravagance of my expectations, the flying experience was bound to be a disappoi…” articula reflexao, life-philosophy, considering em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.

texto: There is no such thing as a beauty contest . . . Beauty is noncompetitive. Beauty of any kind stands alone, unmatched, inimitable, uncontested.
explicacao: Citação de Edna Robinson sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “There is no such thing as a beauty contest . . . Beauty is noncompetitive. Beauty of any kind sta…” articula reflexao, beauty, there em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.

texto: Success, as we categorize it, is a simple and pitiable thing. It's only a matter of degree of wanting, and accident. Wanting plays the major role in everybody's life--accident all the others. The only condition any of us can be sure of in this universe is wanting. How tepid or burning hot the want is depends on accident. But since accident isn't really as accidental as we'd like to think--accident is the great fooler and comforter of mankind--we become 'successful' exactly to the degree we want.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Edna Robinson: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. O trecho “Success, as we categorize it, is a simple and pitiable thing. It's only a matter of degree of wan…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, life-experience, accident pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e reflexao.

But since accident isn't really as accidental as we'd like to think--accident is the great fooler and comforter of mankind--we become 'successful' exactly to the degree we want.
